case_title: LAWTON, JEFFREY VS FOUR STAR PRIVATE CHEFS, INC

docket_entries.description: THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF LAWTON, JEFFREY IS NOT OWED ANY MONEY BY DEFENDANT FOUR STAR PRIVATE CHEFS, INC DEFENDANT APPEARED PERSONALLY AT TRIAL, WITHDREW ITS CHALLENGE TO VENUE, AND WAIVED ANY CLAIM DEFECTS IN SERVICE OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. THE COURT DETERMINES THAT PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE WAS NOT SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH THE EXISTENCE OF A CONTRACT OF EMPLOYMENT FOR A FIXED TERM. STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS
SUBSTITUTED PROOF OF SERVICE FILED ON CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF SERVED AS TO DEFENDANT FOUR STAR PRIVATE CHEFS, INC
DEFT'S VENUE CHALLENGE FILED(VIA MAIL);COPIES(J.C. VENUE FORM+DEFT'S LETTER)MAILED TO ALL PARTIES.
MONEY CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF FOR $5,000.00 FILED BY LAWTON, JEFFREY AS TO DEFENDANT FOUR STAR PRIVATE CHEFS, INC ORDER SETTING TRIAL ISSUED FOR AUG-17-2007 AT 08:30 AM IN DEPT 506
